design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed = hash('Computer-use cache|use_case|builders and operators|2026-07-06T12:15:18.335671+00:00') % 10000 → selected architecture = workflow-command-center. AIDA Check: Hero (Attention), Proof & Market context (Interest), Motion demo & cache explanation (Desire), CTA band (Action) all present. Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk. Hero Math Verification: clamp headline yields 2–3 lines at 1200px with max-width 1120px. Bento Density Verification: compare-grid and bento use grid-auto-flow:dense. GSAP Motion Selection: image-scale-fade-scroll + hover-physics guarded for no-GSAP. Label Sweep & Button Check: all CTAs point to https://app.getsupers.com/general?login=1&open_login=1 with black/white pill contrast.
headline: Computer-use cache for repeated AI agent workflows

site_brief.audience: builders and operators who need repeated computer-use automations to get cheaper and more reliable
site_brief.goal: Create an indexable SEO use-case page in the /use-cases/ cluster. Target the practical query 'Computer-use cache', show the exact job-to-be-done, and explain how Super can perform the workflow with real computer use and a reusable computer-use cache. Include sibling use-case links so the cluster feels intentional, crawlable, and useful. Add a long-form operator playbook with how-to steps, setup checklist, realistic limits, confirmation moments, and examples that a reader can act on immediately.
site_brief.title: Computer-use cache
site_brief.use_case_intent: explain Super's computer-use cache as the reusable memory layer for repeated browser and desktop workflows

summary: A practical operator field guide explaining how Super’s computer-use cache makes repeated browser and desktop automations cheaper, more reliable, and easier to scale than ad‑hoc computer-use agents.
